author: gb9t
content:
Iter Phase C continues — 2 more high-impact Tier-3s (iommufd VFIO compat shim + KVM eventfd):

- drivers/iommu/iommufd-vfio-compat.md: covers iommufd/vfio_compat.c (~540 lines) — legacy VFIO type1 container shim for transition window: when CONFIG_IOMMUFD_VFIO_CONTAINER=y, opening /dev/vfio/vfio routes through iommufd; full VFIO_GET_API_VERSION/CHECK_EXTENSION/SET_IOMMU/IOMMU_GET_INFO/MAP_DMA/UNMAP_DMA/DIRTY_PAGES translation to internal Ioas::map/_unmap + iommufd_hwpt_get_dirty_bitmap, IOMMU_VFIO_IOAS ioctl on /dev/iommu fd for explicit compat-IOAS attach, NOIOMMU mode bridged via no-op pseudo-IOAS w/ CAP_SYS_RAWIO check, errno translation strict per VFIO API spec, performance ≤ 2% overhead vs native iommufd path. 12 REQ / 7 AC / Layer-1 Kani SAFETY (compat-IOAS-UAF + arg-size-OOB + errno-translation-consistent) / Layer-3 invariants / Layer-4 Verus legacy↔modern equivalence.
- virt/kvm/eventfd.md: covers virt/kvm/eventfd.c (~1050 lines) — KVM_IRQFD + KVM_IOEVENTFD bypass mechanisms: eventfd-driven IRQ injection (vhost-net/scsi/vsock writes eventfd → POLLIN callback → kvm_set_irq → LAPIC IRR → guest VMENTRY sees IRQ; bypass userspace round-trip), KVM_IOEVENTFD MMIO/PIO write match → eventfd signal (virtio doorbell bypass), KVM_IRQFD_FLAG_RESAMPLE for level-triggered emulated INTx with resamplefd notify on guest EOI, vfio-pci virqfd integration for HW MSI-X→guest direct delivery, per-VM irqfd + ioeventfd count caps, GSI routing seqlock-protected against KVM_SET_GSI_ROUTING race, per-VM destroy releases all eventfds. 10 REQ / 7 AC / Layer-1 Kani SAFETY (irqfd-UAF + eventfd-UAF + routing-seqlock-consistent + resampler-no-double-ack) / Layer-2 ref to irqfd_lifetime.tla / Layer-3 invariants / Layer-4 Verus signal→IRQ round-trip.

Total state: 274 .design docs, 100 Tier-2 + 40 Tier-3s. Foundation Tier-3 set (40): + iommufd-vfio-compat + kvm-eventfd. Reached the round 40-Tier-3 milestone.

Knowledge pages: design-drivers-iommu-iommufd-vfio-compat, design-virt-kvm-eventfd.

Next iter plan: drivers/iommu/iommufd-viommu.md (virtual IOMMU object + virtual device for nested-guest IOMMU emulation) + virt/kvm/dirty-tracking.md (per-vCPU dirty-ring for live migration acq-rel ordering).
